title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud-based services. It can lead to data encryption, service disruption, financial loss, and reputational damage. Compromised CI/CD pipelines may also result in the spread of malicious code across multiple environments.
recommended_actions: Audit and secure CI/CD pipelines to prevent unauthorized access and code injection.
Implement robust backup strategies to ensure data recovery in case of ransomware attacks.
Deploy advanced threat detection and response solutions to monitor and mitigate suspicious activity in cloud environments.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: A weakness has been identified in usememos memos up to 0.22.1. This affects the function...
description: A weakness has been identified in usememos memos up to 0.22.1. This affects the function memos_access_token of the file src/App.tsx of the component UpdateInstanceSetting. This manipulation of the argument additionalStyle/additionalScript causes improper authorization. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in usememos memos up to version 0.22.1 allows for improper authorization through the manipulation of arguments in the UpdateInstanceSetting component. This could lead to unauthorized access, data leakage, or potential system compromise. The availability of a public exploit increases the risk of widespread attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to a patched version of usememos, if available. If no patch exists, consider temporarily disabling or restricting access to the vulnerable component.
Implement web application firewall (WAF) rules to detect and block malicious payloads targeting the `memos_access_token` function.
Conduct a security audit of all systems using usememos to identify potential unauthorized access or exploitation.

title: A vulnerability was identified in Tenda F451 1.0.0.7_cn_svn7958. The affected element is the...
description: A vulnerability was identified in Tenda F451 1.0.0.7_cn_svn7958. The affected element is the function fromSafeClientFilter of the file /goform/SafeClientFilter of the component httpd. The manipulation of the argument menufacturer/Go leads to buffer overflow. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Tenda F451 firmware allows remote exploitation via buffer overflow, potentially leading to unauthorized access, denial of service, or execution of arbitrary code. The exploit is publicly available, increasing the likelihood of attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the firmware to the latest version if a patch is available.
Restrict remote access to the device and implement network segmentation to limit exposure.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and apply intrusion detection/prevention systems.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Yifang CMS up to 2.0.5. The impacted element is the...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Yifang CMS up to 2.0.5. The impacted element is the function store of the file plugins/yifang_backend_account/logic/admin/L_rbac_admin.php of the component Extended Management Module. The manipulation of the argument Account results in cross site scripting. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The discovered cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in Yifang CMS up to version 2.0.5 allows attackers to execute malicious scripts remotely. This can lead to data theft, session hijacking, or unauthorized access to sensitive information.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Businesses using Yifang CMS
recommended_actions: Immediately update or patch the Yifang CMS to a secure version if available. If no patch exists, consider disabling or restricting access to the vulnerable Extended Management Module.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious payloads targeting this vulnerability.
Conduct a security audit of your CMS installation and monitor for any unusual activity or signs of exploitation.

The manipulation leads to server-side request forgery. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The vendor was contacted earl

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Mogu Blog v2 up to 5.2 allows for Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF), which could enable attackers to manipulate server-side requests, potentially exposing sensitive internal resources, accessing unauthorized data, or enabling further attacks on the infrastructure.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Businesses using Mogu Blog
recommended_actions: Immediately update Mogu Blog to a patched version (if available) or apply any mitigations recommended by the vendor.

title: When sed is invoked with both -i (in-place edit) and --follow-symlinks, the function...
description:
When sed is invoked with both -i (in-place edit) and --follow-symlinks, the function open_next_file() performs two separate, non-atomic filesystem operations on the same path: 
1. resolves symlink to its target and stores the resolved path for determining when output is written,
2. opens the original symlink path (not the resolved one) to read the file. 
Between these two calls there is a race window. If an attacker atomically replaces the symlink with a different target during that window, sed

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5958.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

X.509: Fix out-of-bounds...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

X.509: Fix out-of-bounds access when parsing extensions

Leo reports an out-of-bounds access when parsing a certificate with
empty Basic Constraints or Key Usage extension because the first byte of
the extension is read before checking its length.  Fix it.

The bug can be triggered by an unprivileged user by submitting a
specially crafted certificate to the kernel through the keyrings(7) API.
Leo has demonstrated this with a pr

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-31430.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net: skb: fix cross-cache...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net: skb: fix cross-cache free of KFENCE-allocated skb head

SKB_SMALL_HEAD_CACHE_SIZE is intentionally set to a non-power-of-2
value (e.g. 704 on x86_64) to avoid collisions with generic kmalloc
bucket sizes. This ensures that skb_kfree_head() can reliably use
skb_end_offset to distinguish skb heads allocated from
skb_small_head_cache vs. generic kmalloc caches.

However, when KFENCE is enabled, kfence_ksize() returns the exac

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-31429.

title: Fudo Enterprise in versions from 5.5.0 through 5.6.2 allows low privileged users to access...
description:
Fudo Enterprise in versions from 5.5.0 through 5.6.2 allows low privileged users to access certain administrator-only resources via improperly protected API endpoints. This includes sensitive information such as system logs and parts of system configuration settings.
This vulnerability has been fixed in version 5.6.3

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows low-privileged users to access sensitive administrator-only resources, potentially leading to unauthorized access to system logs and configuration settings. This could result in information disclosure, system misconfigurations, or preparation for further exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade Fudo Enterprise to version 5.6.3 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Review and audit access logs to identify any unauthorized access attempts to sensitive resources.
Implement additional monitoring and alerting for API endpoint activity to detect potential exploitation.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in TransformerOptimus SuperAGI up to 0.0.14. This...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in TransformerOptimus SuperAGI up to 0.0.14. This affects the function extract_with_bs4/extract_with_3k/extract_with_lxml of the file superagi/helper/webpage_extractor.py of the component WebScraperTool. Such manipulation leads to server-side request forgery. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in TransformerOptimus SuperAGI up to version 0.0.14 allows for server-side request forgery (SSRF) attacks, which could enable attackers to manipulate server requests, access sensitive internal resources, or exfiltrate data. The public disclosure of the exploit increases the likelihood of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade TransformerOptimus SuperAGI to a version higher than 0.0.14 if available, or apply any patches provided by the vendor.
Implement input validation and sanitization in the WebScraperTool to mitigate SSRF risks.
Monitor server logs for unusual or unauthorized outbound requests that may indicate exploitation attempts.
